senses.definition: the sport or activity of moving on ice with ice skates

examples.x: She enjoys ice skating as a way to stay active and have fun during the winter months.
Ice skating is a popular pastime for families, with many people enjoying outdoor skating rinks in city parks.
He took up ice skating as a hobby and spends his weekends practicing jumps and spins at the local ice rink.
They decided to go ice skating as a group activity, enjoying the camaraderie and excitement of skating together.
Ice skating is a tradition in their family, with generations of relatives gathering to skate on frozen ponds and lakes.

senses.definition: a competitive ice skating sport where athletes race over a specific distance, aiming to achieve the fastest time

examples.x: Speed skating requires exceptional balance and endurance.
She trained hard to improve her technique in speed skating.
The Olympic gold medalist in speed skating set a new world record.
The speed skating event attracted athletes from around the world.
He started speed skating at a young age and quickly showed promise.

senses.definition: a sport where individuals or pairs perform artistic routines on ice, combining elements of dance, jumps, and spins

examples.x: She practiced figure skating diligently to improve her spins.
Figure skating requires grace, balance, and precision on the ice.
The pair executed a flawless lift during their figure skating routine.
They choreographed a new routine for the upcoming figure skating championship.
Figure skating at the Winter Olympics showcases elite athletes' skills and creativity.

senses.definition: a contact sport played by two teams of roller skaters skating counterclockwise around a track

examples.x: Roller derby players wear protective gear to prevent injuries.
Roller derby matches are full of fast-paced action and excitement.
She joined a roller derby league to pursue her passion for skating.
The roller derby league hosts tournaments throughout the year.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of moving around quickly on skates

examples.x: She enjoys roller skating at the park on weekends.
Roller skating can be a fun way to exercise and improve balance.
He used to go roller skating at the roller disco every Friday night.
They practiced roller skating tricks in the parking lot.

senses.definition: a competitive skating sport where participants race on inline skates, aiming for speed on a track or road course

examples.x: Sarah won first place in the inline speed skating race, crossing the finish line with a burst of speed.
My sister trains hard for inline speed skating competitions to improve her performance.
The inline speed skating event attracted a large crowd of spectators cheering for the racers.
The inline speed skating team practices together every week to refine their skills and build camaraderie.
Tyler's passion for inline speed skating began when he was just a child, gliding effortlessly along the pavement.

senses.definition: a type of skating that is performed on paved surfaces like streets or roads, often using inline skates or rollerblades

examples.x: Road skating requires balance and coordination to navigate uneven surfaces and obstacles.
Road skating events and races attract participants from around the world.
Many cities have designated lanes or paths for road skating enthusiasts.
Speed is a thrill of road skating, but it's important to skate responsibly and follow traffic rules.
We enjoy road skating on weekends, exploring different neighborhoods and parks.

senses.definition: a team ice skating sport where a group of skaters performs choreographed routines together

examples.x: The synchronized skating team practiced their routine tirelessly to perfect their timing.
Sarah joined the synchronized skating club to enjoy the sport with her friends.
Watching synchronized skating at the competition was mesmerizing as the skaters moved in perfect harmony.
Synchronized skating requires strong communication among team members to execute complex formations flawlessly.
During the synchronized skating performance, the audience cheered loudly for the team's synchronized spins and jumps.

senses.definition: a sport involving various activities using inline skates, which have two to five wheels arranged in a single line under a boot

examples.x: Inline skating clubs often organize group skates and social events.
Inline skating helps improve cardiovascular health and leg strength.
Advanced inline skating techniques include jumps, spins, and tricks.
Inline skating is popular among all age groups for its versatility and fun.
Inline skating competitions often include speed races and freestyle events.

senses.definition: a sport where skaters perform intricate tricks and maneuvers around a series of evenly spaced cones on inline skates

examples.x: Freestyle slalom skating requires precision and creativity to navigate the cones smoothly.
Many skaters practice freestyle slalom skating to showcase their agility and skills.
Lisa loves freestyle slalom skating because it combines artistry with athleticism.
Freestyle slalom skating is popular at skate parks where skaters can set up their own cone patterns.
Mastering freestyle slalom skating takes hours of dedicated practice and patience.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of performing choreographed routines on roller skates, combining dance and acrobatics

examples.x: Maria excels in artistic roller skating, impressing judges with her graceful routines.
Artistic roller skating routines are often set to music, enhancing the performance.
Artistic roller skating combines elements of dance and gymnastics on skates.
The audience was captivated by the artistic roller skating team's synchronized movements.
Lily practices artistic roller skating daily to perfect her spins and jumps.

senses.definition: the sport or activity of riding a skateboard

examples.x: He spends most of his free time practicing skateboarding, honing his skills at the local skate park.

senses.definition: a form of skateboarding that involves performing tricks and maneuvers on urban streets, sidewalks, and obstacles like rails and stairs

examples.x: The skateboarder showcased impressive skills during a street skateboarding session downtown.
Jake practices street skateboarding in his neighborhood park after school.
Street skateboarding often involves navigating through city landscapes and using obstacles creatively.
Sarah enjoys watching street skateboarding videos online for inspiration.
Street skateboarding competitions feature riders showcasing their best tricks on urban courses.

senses.definition: a form of skateboarding that involves performing tricks and maneuvers in specially designed skate parks with ramps, rails, and obstacles

examples.x: Park skateboarding requires skill and creativity to navigate ramps and obstacles.
Park skateboarding sessions often involve collaborating with friends to learn new tricks.
Park skateboarding is a thrilling way to push the limits and progress as a skater.
Sam enjoys practicing park skateboarding tricks at the local skate park.
Park skateboarding videos on social media inspire others to try new tricks.

senses.definition: a type of skateboarding that involves performing tricks and maneuvers on a skateboard in a vertical or ramp-based setting, such as halfpipes or skateparks

examples.x: Tony Hawk is famous for his vertical skateboarding skills on halfpipes.
The skateboarders at the X Games wowed the crowd with their vertical skateboarding prowess.
Vertical skateboarding competitions showcase impressive aerial stunts and tricks.
Watching vertical skateboarding videos online inspired Emily to try it herself.
Sarah practiced vertical skateboarding at the local skatepark to improve her technique.

senses.definition: a type of skateboarding using longer boards, typically for cruising, downhill racing, or freestyle riding
a position in figure skating where the skater glides with legs extended outward and straight, resembling the shape of an eagle's wings

examples.x: She gracefully performed a spread eagle across the ice.
His spread eagle displayed impressive flexibility.
The skater transitioned into a spread eagle before the jump.
She practiced holding a strong spread eagle position.
The spread eagle is a classic move in figure skating.

senses.definition: a skating jump that takes off from a forward outside edge and includes one-and-a-half revolutions in the air before landing

examples.x: Emily practiced her Axel jump tirelessly to perfect its execution.
Tom's favorite jump to perform in his routine is the Axel.
Mark struggled to land the Axel during his figure skating routine.
Performing an Axel in sync with music adds flair to the routine.
The Axel is one of the most challenging jumps in figure skating due to its technical requirements.

senses.definition: a skateboarding trick in which the skater rotates the board around its axis while in the air, often involving flipping the board with their feet

examples.x: John practiced his flip tricks at the skate park to improve his skills.
Sarah landed a perfect kickflip, a popular flip trick in skateboarding.
Jake's favorite part of skateboarding is experimenting with new flip tricks.
Emily enjoys watching videos of skaters performing stylish flip tricks online.

senses.definition: a skateboarding trick where the skater uses their foot to flip the board in the air while jumping, causing it to rotate horizontally before landing back on the ground

examples.x: Tony practiced the kickflip for hours until he finally landed it perfectly.
Sarah's favorite trick to do on her skateboard is the kickflip.
It took Liam several attempts, but he finally managed to land the kickflip without falling.
The kickflip is a fundamental trick in street skateboarding.
Watching professional skateboarders perform kickflips effortlessly is inspiring.

senses.definition: (skateboarding) the act of riding backward while maintaining the normal stance on the board

examples.x: Practicing fakie helps improve overall skateboarding skills.
She landed the jump perfectly and continued rolling fakie.
She skated fakie through the entire course during the competition.
Transitioning to fakie mid-run can surprise opponents.

senses.definition: a skateboarding trick where the rider leaps into the air without using their hands by popping the board off the ground with their feet

examples.x: Jake practiced his ollie for hours to perfect his jump.
She landed her first ollie and felt an amazing sense of achievement.
Jessica's high ollie impressed everyone watching at the competition.
With a powerful ollie, he cleared the entire set of stairs effortlessly.
After weeks of trying, Liam finally nailed his ollie on his new skateboard.

senses.definition: a skateboarding trick where the rider uses the front foot to press down the nose of the board and lift the rear wheels off the ground

examples.x: Learning a nollie can be challenging for beginner skateboarders.
The nollie is a foundational trick for many advanced skateboarding maneuvers.
Kevin's favorite trick is the nollie because it looks so smooth.
You need to shift your weight forward to execute a nollie correctly.
The skateboarder performed a nollie over the rail effortlessly.

senses.definition: a skateboarding maneuver that involves riding or performing tricks with the opposite stance from a skater's natural or usual stance

examples.x: Jake practiced riding switch to improve his overall skateboarding skills.
Mastering switch in skateboarding takes time and patience.
The skateboarding competition had a special round for switch.
She landed a perfect switch on her first try.

senses.definition: a skateboarding trick where the board flips forward beneath the skater's feet using the heel of the leading foot

examples.x: She nailed the heelflip first try.
She's been practicing her heelflips all afternoon.
He landed a clean heelflip over the stairs.
He popped a huge heelflip off the curb.
She's determined to land a heelflip down the big set.
